findings.quote: One national operator after another quietly ___(1)___ its overnight services
findings.note: question style: numbered-gap vocabulary cloze with 'Gap (n)' prompts and single-word options (dismissed/declined/withdrew/renounced) is a Use-of-English format alien to HP ELF; the section is reading-comprehension only (cf. exemplars var-2022-1-ELF-031, host-2021-ELF-040), so this is wrong-section genre, not an ELF passage.

comparative_note: Under the amended cloze-format scope rule (owner 2026-07-24) the gap format is out of jurisdiction; judged on in-scope dimensions the passage holds its own beside the exemplars. Genre/source-plausibility is magazine-grade: a signed transport column with real-world residue (Halden Rail, the Aurora service, carriages leased from an operator two countries away, a named economist Ingrid Sollander with an audit and a direct quote), matching the National Geographic History texture of host-2021-verb2-ELF-040 rather than manufactured filler. Intellectual temperature is genuinely adult and complicated: it stages nostalgia/subsidy against arithmetic ("the romance of the sleeper... has for the moment outrun its arithmetic"), the same settled-fact-resisting tension as the OECD-spending and mouse-funding argument items var-2022-1-verb1-ELF-031 and host-ver2-2019-verb1-ELF-031. Option-word register (underwrite/condone/overturn/dispel; auspicious/precarious/gregarious/meticulous) is educated-adult and congruent with the prose, with no listicle tidiness, reader address, or SEO shaping. Indistinguishable in kind from the exemplars; supersedes the prior kill, which rested solely on the cloze format now excluded from this gate.
